tax: Placed at genus rank, Galeb Dur is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: an upright, self-righting granite body with fault-seam features. A new species belongs here only if it shows a walking solid-rock body of the granite type.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a hunched boulder on two stub-legs. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — paired flat pressure prints a metre apart — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Galeb Dur occupies granite scree-benches. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where fault pressure saturates a boulder-pile and recur with tremor re-wakes the body, sustaining themselves on tectonic pressure.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: poses as terrain, rises to block and crush. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: two-tonne granite mass and tremor-sense. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each species and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: stone-fusing and boulder-rolling. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Galeb Dur matters because the commonest fatal 'rockfall' that walked. It lets survey reports sort walking boulders from rolling ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Galeb Dur is the genus-rank grouping of walking solid-boulder elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share an upright two-legged granite body that fuses stone to heal, and divide below into species groups by size and the fault system that wakes them. The classic 'walking boulder' of the lower gorge.
